title: Unused private types or members should be removed
description: This rule raises an issue when a private/internal type or member is never referenced in the code. A type or member that is never called is dead code, and should be removed. Cleaning out dead code decreases the size of the maintained codebase, making it easier to understand the program and preventing bugs from being introduced.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: Unused private type or member should be removed

logic.reasoning: Private members that are declared but never used are dead code. They add maintenance burden and confusion. However, serialization attributes, event handlers, and framework callbacks may appear unused but are invoked by the framework.
